meta.name: Sleep & Cognitive Performance
meta.description: Demo chain: causal model of sleep quality and cognitive outcomes.

nodes.label: Poor sleep quality
nodes.description: Consistently less than 7h or fragmented sleep

nodes.label: Elevated cortisol
nodes.description: Chronic sleep debt raises baseline cortisol

nodes.label: Reduced focus
nodes.description: Difficulty sustaining attention on demanding tasks

nodes.label: Working memory impairment
nodes.description: Reduced capacity to hold and manipulate information

nodes.label: Regular aerobic exercise
nodes.description: 30+ min moderate cardio reduces cortisol and improves sleep quality

nodes.label: Poor decision quality
nodes.description: Increased error rate on complex tasks

nodes.label: cold swim
nodes.description: Brief cold water immersion (~3 min) that acutely and transiently lowers cortisol and triggers norepinephrine release.

nodes.label: norepinephrine spike
nodes.description: Acute surge in norepinephrine following cold exposure, proposed to prime attention circuits and transiently improve focus.

nodes.label: time-limited effect window
nodes.description: The focus-enhancing effect of cold exposure is bounded to approximately 3 hours, indicating a transient rather than sustained causal pathway.

nodes.label: Stressor Exposure
nodes.description: An organism or system encounters a demand or threat that exceeds or challenges its current capacity.

nodes.label: Stress Appraisal
nodes.description: Cognitive or systemic evaluation of whether a demand is threatening, harmful, or benign relative to available resources.

nodes.label: Physiological Stress Response
nodes.description: Activation of neuroendocrine pathways (e.g., HPA axis, sympathetic nervous system) producing cortisol, adrenaline, and arousal.

nodes.label: Psychological Stress State
nodes.description: Subjective experience of tension, anxiety, or overwhelm arising from appraised demand-resource imbalance.

nodes.label: Coping Resources
nodes.description: Individual or collective capacities (social support, skills, resilience) that moderate the impact of stressors.

nodes.label: Allostatic Load
nodes.description: Cumulative physiological wear and tear resulting from chronic or repeated stress activation beyond adaptive capacity.

nodes.label: Performance Modulation
nodes.description: Stress follows an inverted-U relationship with performance: moderate arousal enhances it, excess degrades it (Yerkes-Dodson principle).

nodes.label: Health Outcome Degradation
nodes.description: Chronic stress contributes to immune suppression, cardiovascular disease, mental health disorders, and metabolic dysfunction.

nodes.label: Adaptive Growth
nodes.description: Acute manageable stress (eustress) can trigger learning, resilience building, and post-traumatic growth.

nodes.label: Context Domain
nodes.description: The specific named domain, entity, or setting in which stress occurs (e.g., workplace, clinical trial, geopolitical event) — instance value unknown without ΔDATA.

nodes.label: cold swim / cold water immersion
nodes.description: Acute cold exposure (e.g. 3-minute cold swim) that transiently suppresses cortisol and triggers a norepinephrine spike

nodes.label: norepinephrine spike
nodes.description: Acute catecholamine release triggered by cold exposure that primes attention circuits

nodes.label: attentional sharpness / sustained focus
nodes.description: Subjectively noticeable improvement in focused attention lasting ~3 hours post cold exposure, distinct from working memory capacity

nodes.label: cold swim
nodes.description: 3-minute cold water immersion practice performed in the morning that triggers acute physiological stress responses

nodes.label: acute cortisol reduction
nodes.description: Transient lowering of cortisol levels caused by cold exposure, distinct from chronic cortisol elevation

nodes.label: sustained focus window
nodes.description: Period of noticeably sharper attentional focus lasting approximately 3 hours following cold exposure

nodes.label: intervention strategy gate
nodes.description: Formal decision fork between cold swim (Path A: cost-efficient) and L-tyrosine + ashwagandha stack (Path B: time-efficient), remaining open until the 4-week cold swim trial concludes.

nodes.label: L-tyrosine + ashwagandha stack
nodes.description: Supplement stack taken on waking as a time-cheap alternative to cold swim, intended to replicate norepinephrine spike and acute cortisol reduction at recurring monetary cost.

nodes.label: 4-week cold swim trial
nodes.description: Time-bounded four-week trial of the cold swim protocol whose completion closes the intervention strategy gate and determines which strategy is selected.

nodes.label: 90-min cognitive peak
nodes.description: Desired terminal state: focus and working memory above baseline for 90 uninterrupted minutes every morning.

nodes.label: Attentional resource theory
nodes.description: The brain allocates a finite executive-bandwidth pool; norepinephrine is the primary modulator of pool size.

nodes.label: Focus window exceeds 90 min?
nodes.description: Unresolved: does the focus-window reliably extend past 90 min under chronic protocol, or does receptor downregulation cap the effect?

nodes.label: HPA axis calibration variance
nodes.description: Opaque mechanism causing day-to-day noise in cortisol-reduction magnitude; internal process unknown.

nodes.label: Biometric measurement protocol
nodes.description: Decision to record resting HRV and a 3-min reaction-time benchmark every morning to generate evidence for resolving the open question.

nodes.label: HRV chest strap
nodes.description: Validated HRV sensor (Polar H10 or equivalent, ≥95% accuracy vs. ECG reference).

nodes.label: Cognitive benchmark app
nodes.description: Standardised daily cognitive test with score export; required for reaction-time series data.

nodes.label: cold water access ≤15°C
nodes.description: Physical access to cold water at or below 15°C within 30 minutes of waking, serving as a hard prerequisite for executing the cold swim task.

nodes.label: ice bag supply (≥2kg per session)
nodes.description: Secondary asset used in summer to cool tap water to ≤15°C when ambient tap temperature exceeds the cold swim protocol threshold.

nodes.label: cold water source discovery task
nodes.description: Upstream task triggered when primary cold water access is absent, aimed at identifying or establishing a viable cold water source meeting the ≤15°C requirement.

nodes.label: intervention strategy gate
nodes.description: Formal decision fork between Path A (cold swim protocol) and Path B (L-tyrosine + ashwagandha stack), scoring each on cost and time-efficiency, remaining open until the 4-week cold swim trial concludes.

nodes.label: L-tyrosine + ashwagandha stack (Path B)
nodes.description: Pharmacological intervention combining low-dose L-tyrosine with ashwagandha taken on waking; competing strategy to Path A targeting norepinephrine spike and cortisol reduction; recurring cost, time-cheap.

nodes.label: norepinephrine spike
nodes.description: Acute elevation of norepinephrine serving as the shared proximal neurochemical output targeted by both intervention paths.

nodes.label: acute cortisol reduction
nodes.description: Short-term decrease in cortisol following the chosen intervention, modelled as downstream of the norepinephrine spike.

nodes.label: sustained focus window
nodes.description: Extended period of heightened cognitive focus produced downstream of the norepinephrine spike and cortisol reduction; the intended end-state outcome of the intervention strategies.

edges.evidence: The focus window must be measurably present before the 90-min goal can be claimed achieved.
Until the 90-min ceiling question is resolved, the goal cannot be confirmed achievable via this pathway.
Attentional resource theory explains why the spike increases executive pool size.
Theory predicts the focus window is the interval of elevated pool capacity.
Unknown calibration variance causes day-to-day noise in cortisol-reduction magnitude.
HRV measurement cannot proceed without a validated chest-strap sensor.
Reaction-time series requires a standardised benchmark app with daily export.
Daily biometric series will determine whether the 90-min window is a ceiling or a floor.
